ocr.text: The analysis presented in the accompanying data table provides a comprehensive overview of the model fit for a single factor
within the studied framework. The metrics outlined, including the chi-square statistic (χ2) and the root mean square error of
approximation (RMSEA), are critical in assessing the adequacy of the model representation.
χ2 (df)  RMSEA  CFI  Factor correlation
One factor
With residual correlation items 1 and 2  3457.229 (26)*  0.075 (0.073–0.078)  0.983
Two factorsa (somatic vs non-somatic)
With residual correlation items 1 and 2  3130.413 (25)*  0.073 (0.071–0.075)  0.984  0.946
Two factors based on exploratory factor analysisb
With residual correlation items 1 and 2  1130.192 (24)*  0.045 (0.042–0.047)  0.994  0.875
Specifically, the χ2 statistic, alongside its degrees of freedom (df), serves as a fundamental test of the model's goodness-of-fit,
indicating how well the proposed model aligns with the observed data. A lower RMSEA value suggests a better fit, with values
below 0.06 typically considered indicative of a satisfactory model. Additionally, the comparative fit index (CFI) is included as a
benchmark for evaluating model performance, with values approaching 1.0 denoting superior fit. Furthermore, the table
presents factor correlation, which elucidates the relationships between latent constructs and provides insight into the underlying
structure of the data. Collectively, these metrics not only inform the reliability and validity of the single factor but also guide
future research directions by highlighting areas for potential model enhancement and refinement.
